settings.title: Wash and chop the mangoes
settings.description: Wash 900g of unripe green mangoes thoroughly. Don't peel — the skin holds the wild lactic acid bacteria you need. Chop into 2cm cubes.

settings.title: Salt and let them sweat
settings.description: In a large bowl, mix the chopped mangoes with 20g salt. Massage gently for 2 minutes. Let sit 30 minutes.

settings.title: Add the spice mix
settings.description: Add the mustard seeds, red chilli, fennel, turmeric, and fenugreek. Mix thoroughly. Add 1 tsp of Ferment Boost.

settings.title: Pack into the jar
settings.description: Transfer into a clean fermenter jar. Press down hard to release brine. Top up with filtered water if needed. Place a glass weight on top.

settings.title: Seal and wait
settings.description: Attach the airlock lid. Place the jar in a cool spot (20–25°C). Within 24 hours you'll see bubbles forming.

settings.title: Taste from day 5
settings.description: On day 5, taste a small piece. If tangy and crunchy, transfer to the fridge. Once refrigerated, keeps 2–3 months.

settings.text: For a milder version: reduce red chilli to 3g and skip fenugreek.
For a sweeter take: add 1 tsp jaggery powder along with the salt — note this slows fermentation by ~1 day.
If you see white film on the surface (kahm yeast), skim it off and continue. It's harmless.
Don't use iodised salt or chlorinated tap water — both kill the bacteria you want.

settings.subtitle: Lacto-fermented raw mango achaar with mustard, fennel and turmeric. No oil. Real probiotics. Ready in 5 days.
settings.kit_desc: Our kit comes with pre-measured spice mixes, the right jar, an airlock, and a glass weight. Open the box, follow the recipe — done.
